methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham, has raised $380M to date against a stated valuation exceeding $1.0B. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of nine companies in the same sector and stage cohort, including eight private firms (KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Amogy at $700M, Redwood Materials at $6.0B, Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B) and one public comparable (Lanzatech, LNZA).

Two triangulation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to next-stage transitions against the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set yields a $500M median, with observed valuations ranging from $120M to $6.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in commercial maturity and capital intensity within the climate-tech cohort.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $425M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The current stated valuation of $1.0B+ sits at the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include product commercialization timeline, customer concentration risk, and sectoral capital availability. The peer-median method carries elevated uncertainty given the substantial valuation variance across comparable private financings.
